---
title: "Logistic Model Selection"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---


```{python}
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

from config import data_path, data_output_path
from src.selection.logit_model_selection import (
    create_stratified_folds_without_sklearn,
    evaluate_saved_score_model,
    generate_logit_formulas,
    load_score_model_pickle,
    save_best_model_pickle,
    train_models_with_all_checks,
)
```


```{python}
path_datasets = data_path
```


```{python}
train_dis = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/train_discretized.csv")
test_dis = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/test_discretized.csv")
oot_dis = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/oot_discretized.csv")

train_dis["def"].value_counts(normalize=True)

F1_score = 2 * (train_dis["def"].value_counts(normalize=True)[1]) / (
    train_dis["def"].value_counts(normalize=True)[1]
    + 1
)
print(f"Baseline F1-score: {F1_score:.4f}")
```


```{python}
variables = [
    "person_income_dis",
    "person_emp_length_dis",
    "loan_int_rate_dis",
    "loan_percent_income_dis",
    "cb_person_default_on_file",
    "home_ownership_3"
]

target = "def"
fold_col = "fold"
n_splits = 4
recall_threshold = 0.5
```


```{python}
train_dis_with_folds = create_stratified_folds_without_sklearn(
    train_df=train_dis,
    n_splits=n_splits,
    target=target,
    year_col="year",
    fold_col=fold_col,
    random_state=42
)
train_dis_with_folds[fold_col].value_counts().sort_index()
```


```{python}
summaries = {}

for k_variables in range(1, len(variables) + 1):
    formulas_k = generate_logit_formulas(
        train_df=train_dis_with_folds.drop(columns=[fold_col]),
        variables=variables,
        k=k_variables,
        target=target
    )

    results_k = train_models_with_all_checks(
        formula_combinations=formulas_k,
        train_df_with_folds=train_dis_with_folds,
        test_df=test_dis,
        oot_df=oot_dis,
        variables=variables,
        target=target,
        fold_col=fold_col,
        alpha=0.05,
        vif_threshold=10,
        recall_threshold=recall_threshold,
        maxiter=100
    )

    summaries[k_variables] = results_k.sort_values(
        [
            "all_checks_OK",
            "Gini_penalized",
            "PR_AUC_penalized",
            "Recall_penalized",
            "F1_penalized",
            "Gini_folds_mean"
        ],
        ascending=[False, False, False, False, False, False]
    )

    print(f"k={k_variables}: {summaries[k_variables].shape[0]} trained models")
```


```{python}
output_file = f"{data_output_path}/summary_models.xlsx"

with pd.ExcelWriter(output_file, engine="openpyxl") as writer:
    for k_variables, summary_df in summaries.items():
        summary_df.to_excel(
            writer,
            sheet_name=f"summary_{k_variables}",
            index=False
        )

output_file
```

## Save and validate the selected 4-variable model

Statistical note: the train Gini is useful as an in-sample diagnostic, but the model validation should mainly rely on the test and OOT Gini/ROC curves. The train metric can be optimistic because the model was fitted on that sample.

```{python}
model_output_dir = Path(data_output_path) / "model_selection"
model_output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

best_4_model_pickle = model_output_dir / "best_4_variables_logit_scorecard.pkl"

best_4_artifact = save_best_model_pickle(
    summary_df=summaries[4],
    train_df=train_dis,
    output_path=best_4_model_pickle,
    n_variables=4,
    target=target,
    maxiter=100
)

best_4_model_pickle
```

```{python}
pd.DataFrame(best_4_artifact["scorecard_table"])
```

```{python}
loaded_best_4_artifact = load_score_model_pickle(best_4_model_pickle)

gini_summary, roc_curves = evaluate_saved_score_model(
    artifact=loaded_best_4_artifact,
    datasets={
        "Train": train_dis,
        "Test": test_dis,
        "OOT": oot_dis,
    },
    target=target
)

gini_summary
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 5))

for dataset_name, roc_df in roc_curves.items():
    gini_value = gini_summary.loc[
        gini_summary["dataset"].eq(dataset_name),
        "Gini"
    ].iloc[0]

    ax.plot(
        roc_df["fpr"],
        roc_df["tpr"],
        label=f"{dataset_name} - Gini={gini_value:.2%}"
    )

ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--", color="grey", linewidth=1)
ax.set_title("ROC curves - selected 4-variable model")
ax.set_xlabel("False Positive Rate")
ax.set_ylabel("True Positive Rate")
ax.legend(loc="lower right")
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
```


```{python}
summaries[1].head(20)
```


```{python}
summaries[2].head(20)
```
